entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal grain species, Pennisetum glaucum.

entities.name: DIVERSITY ARRAYS TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: A genotyping technology used for genetic analysis, abbreviated as DArT.

entities.name: DART MARKERS
entities.description: Genetic markers generated using Diversity Arrays Technology.

entities.name: INBRED LINES
entities.description: Genetically uniform lines derived from self-pollination.

entities.name: RIL POPULATIONS
entities.description: Recombinant Inbred Lines, derived from crossing two inbred lines and selfing for several generations.

entities.name: QTL MAPPING
entities.description: A statistical method to identify genomic regions associated with quantitative traits.

entities.name: GENETIC LINKAGE MAPS
entities.description: Maps showing the relative positions of genetic markers.

entities.name: RUST RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to resist infection by rust pathogens.

entities.name: LG 1
entities.description: A specific linkage group, designated as 1.

entities.name: RESISTANCE GENES
entities.description: Genes conferring resistance to diseases or pests.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding technique using genetic markers to accelerate the recovery of the recurrent parent genome.

entities.name: RECURRENT PARENT
entities.description: The parent plant whose genome is largely recovered during backcrossing.

entities.name: DONOR PARENT
entities.description: The parent plant contributing the desired trait during introgression.

relationships.weight: DArT technology is used in the study of pearl millet.
DArT markers are mapped onto genetic linkage maps.
Genetic linkage maps are used to assess diversity in inbred lines.
Genetic linkage maps are used for QTL mapping.
QTL mapping is used to identify loci associated with rust resistance.
The rust resistance locus is located on LG 1.
Marker-assisted backcrossing is used to recover the recurrent parent genome.
